approach: Measure the corpus before accepting the issue's premise: the issue presumes a producer articulates a trade narrative; if no rubric records one, the dominant failure is a trade never said at all and the framing shifts.
Test the named candidate 2 (escalate-when-you-cannot-classify) against the rule as written rather than assuming it reaches; record the rejection so it is not re-checked.
Adopt the procedural record (candidate 1) as the completion of the rule's own say-it-out-loud form, not as a new mechanism; check the FORM of the trade claim, never its truth.
Adopt the residual (candidate 3) explicitly: the bar cannot catch a producer who writes down a trade it does not believe; name the cold judge as the backstop and stop looking.
Scope to authoring only; the existing unrecorded corpus is 282's queue and threshold re-derivation is 294. Freeze a scenario asserting the check does not fire on an existing unrecorded dimension.

what: The spec-producer's completeness precondition enumerates the judged authoring bars whose absence lets the producer report complete, and that enumeration omits both the substitutability selection rule and the trade-recording duty. A suite summing a criterion no strength elsewhere pays for satisfies it, and the producer reports complete. Pre-existing: created when the selection rule landed and inherited unswept. Adding a conjunct to the frozen precondition is a narrowing that fires Clearance, so it needs owner ratification and cannot be folded into an additive CR.

Three rubric blocks in two corpus nodes use a retired inline dimension syntax instead of the prescribed named-dimension form. The rule is old, universal, and enforced by the judge rather than the mechanical form check; one of the two files is frozen, was touched the same day, and is still nonconforming. Evidence that a structural duty no linter reads drifts unnoticed.

Eight of the thirty-seven rubric scenarios set the cut equal to the sum of every dimension's maximum, so no dimension can be traded at all and each must score its maximum to pass. This is confounded with the cut being a legitimate policy call a spec owner may set strictly, so it is not a defect on its face. Routed as a signal for the corpus re-derivation queue, not as a rule: deriving substitutability from the cut's arithmetic is the move the selection rule exists to refuse.

The duty requiring a recorded reason for a rubric's cut was added to the bar without a frozen scenario binding it to any producer or judge behavior. A duty argued in prose and never frozen is not specified. This CR folds the trade record into that same record, so both would be bound by one scenario.

Lint the presence of the record carrying a rubric's cut reason and each authored dimension's trade. Presence is a lexical fact a diff-aware form check reads more reliably than a judge, and this CR deliberately leaves the duty with no judge-side enforcement because making a missing record judge-reportable narrows a frozen scenario that is silent on records. The lint must follow the corpus backfill, not precede it: run today it would fire on roughly a hundred standing dimensions that are the backfill's own ground.

The trade record's syntax is unspecified and its impl-gate consumers are unswept. The bar says which record carries the trade but not the form it takes, and only the spec-judge is told to ignore it; the judges that read a scenario's inline rubric at the impl gate are told nothing about it. Benign while the record is prose that no schema parser reads, but it is an unswept consumer set and the natural home for the record-presence lint once the corpus backfill lands.

why.floor: none — both touched suites classify addOnly (4/0/0 and 4/0/0), freeze self-clears, no frozen scenario edited; three floors were found and closed during the grill (R1 Conflict, R2/R3 Clearance carried by prose the freeze cannot read), each on the same negative-Then frozen scenario, and the final rework adds zero new selection-failure ground so none can reach it
why.blast: low — a producer-only recording duty the judge never grades, plus a doctrine section; the judge's behavior on any dimension is byte-identical to origin/main
why.novelty: low — no new floor and no new route; the escalate-into-selection rule the new judge scenarios freeze already existed in origin/main prose
why.confidence: high — four judge rounds, three adversarial charged FOR the floors; the final pass verified the just-added scenario asserts the same verdict F1 already gives (origin/main cannot pass that rubric) and the record-prohibition smuggles no read-duty; verify 21/21, both features classify additive

why.floor: none — additive to already-implemented nodes, freeze preserved
why.blast: low — governance prose realizing the frozen contract; the judge's behavior is byte-identical to origin/main
why.novelty: none — the contract was settled at the spec gate and the implementation adds no route of its own
why.confidence: high — cold impl-judge re-derived all 8 oracles independently and passed each, verified the producer-writes/judge-ignores split across all five implementing files with no drift, and confirmed the mirrors neither over- nor under-claim; verify 21/21
